titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Jacob Blanck Papers
titlestmt.author: by Tom Hyry

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists primarily of corrected page proofs and a small amount of correspondence created and compiled during the publication of the fourth edition of Merle Johnson's American First Editions (1942).

persname.part: Johnson, Merle De Vore, 1874-1935. American first editions.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Jacob Blanck Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Jacob Blanck Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

bioghist.head: JACOB BLANCK (1906-1974)
bioghist.p: Jacob Blanck's career as a bibliographer and editor included positions as rare book editor of Publishers Weekly and Antiquarian Bookman , 1936-1952; bibliographer in Americana at the Library of Congress, 1939-1941; and editor of Bibliography of American Literature , 1943-1974. He also edited the third and fourth volumes of Merle Johnson's American First Editions (1936, 1942) and published four books, including two children's books. He held an honorary degree of Doctor of Letters from Brown University (1969) and a Phi Beta Kappa from Harvard University (1970). Blanck died December 25, 1974 in Jamaica Plain, Massachusetts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists primarily of corrected page proofs and a small amount of correspondence created and compiled during the publication of the fouth edition of Merle Johnson's American First Editions (1942), for which Blanck served as editor. The papers are arranged alphabetically by author's name.

During the editing process, Blanck sent page proofs of bibliographies to living authors, with a request for their additions and corrections. Many authors returned edited proofs along with cover letters. These instances are noted in the container list. The page proofs of other bibliographies were edited by different individuals, most commonly Carrol A. Wilson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Richard J. Walsh, President of The John Day Company, Inc., Publishers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Edwin H. Carpenter, Jr. Does not include page proof

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with James Henle, President of The Vanguard Press

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from Carroll A. Wilson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from the author's secretary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from James T. Babb

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from the author but no page proofs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Carroll A. Wilson
